title: pedicularis rostratospicata subsp. helvetica
intro: Pedicularis rostratospicata subsp. helvetica is a subspecies of the Pedicularis rostratospicata plant, also known as the lousewort, a flowering plant that belongs to the borage family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pedicularis rostratospicata subsp. helvetica are attributed to its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water or using it as a tincture, and it can be consumed as a tea or added to herbal blends for its medicinal and health benefits.
